U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack (#engineering) using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "expense categoriser" or "expense auto-categoriser" or "auto-categorisation" or "remediation") to discover Samira's original project kickoff, the remediation strategy post, task assignments (Lena: core engine + pipeline hardening, Dmitri: audit + edge cases, Anh: dashboard), and the timeline Samira set for re-enabling auto-decisions (earliest week of 3/30, per Samira's remediation strategy message).
OE 2: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "expense categoriser" or "auto-categorisation" or "remediation" or "pilot") to discover Elena Rostova's CEO directive suspending auto-approve/deny, Samira's remediation plan email with feasible timelines, and Samira's audit summary email reporting 47 expenses reviewed (39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals).
OE 3: Search Linear using `linear_list_issues` (query: "expense" or project: "proj_expense_autocategoriser") to discover all expense categoriser project tickets: ENG-201 through ENG-220, including the P1 config fixes (ENG-214 Vectral, ENG-215 Mosaic), the pipeline hardening ticket (ENG-217), the dashboard type validation ticket (ENG-218), the edge case coverage ticket (ENG-212), the audit ticket (ENG-216), the duplicate receipt detection ticket (ENG-220), and the launch readiness checklist (ENG-219).
OE 4: Retrieve Linear issue details for the pipeline hardening ticket using `linear_get_issue` (id: issue_pipeline_hardening / ENG-217) to check its current state. Assigned to Lena, state: in_progress, due 3/20. Dmitri's prompt says Lena was supposed to incorporate edge case validation tests into this work but he hasn't seen confirmation. Cross-reference with Samira's audit email (which notes "Pipeline hardening, Lena on schedule for Thursday") and Dmitri's comment on ENG-217 posting the full audit results on 3/20 to determine if pipeline hardening is actually complete or stalled.
OE 5: Retrieve Linear issue details for the Vectral config fix ticket using `linear_get_issue` (id: issue_p1_vectral_config / ENG-214) to check its current state. Assigned to Lena, state: in_progress, due 3/18. The prompt references Lena "closing out the Vectral and Mosaic config fixes", verify whether the ticket status has been updated to done or is still showing in_progress despite the work being completed.
OE 6: Retrieve Linear issue details for the Mosaic config fix ticket using `linear_get_issue` (id: issue_p1_mosaic_malformed / ENG-215) to check its current state. Assigned to Lena, state: in_progress, due 3/18. Same verification as OE 5, check whether the fix was deployed but the ticket was never closed.
OE 7: Retrieve Linear issue details for the edge case coverage ticket using `linear_get_issue` (id: issue_edge_cases_backlog / ENG-212) to check its current state. Assigned to Dmitri, state: in_progress, due 3/20. Dmitri says he "finished the audit weeks ago and filed the validation tests" for this ticket, verify whether the validation tests are reflected in the ticket status or comments, and whether they have been integrated into the deployment gate.
OE 8: Retrieve Linear issue details for the dashboard type validation ticket using `linear_get_issue` (id: issue_dashboard_type_validation / ENG-218) to check its current state. Assigned to Anh, state: in_progress, due 3/21. Dmitri says "Anh acknowledged the dashboard type validation in Slack when Samira assigned it during remediation planning, but there's been zero progress on the ticket since", verify this stall and flag it.
OE 9: Retrieve Linear issue details for the audit ticket using `linear_get_issue` (id: issue_audit_expenses / ENG-216) to confirm it is completed. Assigned to Dmitri, state: in_progress, due 3/19. Dmitri says he "finished the audit weeks ago", if this ticket is still in_progress, it needs to be moved to done.
OE 10: Retrieve Linear issue details for the duplicate receipt detection ticket using `linear_get_issue` (id: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection / ENG-220) to check its current state. Assigned to Dmitri, state: todo, due 3/25. Dmitri says "my duplicate receipt detection ticket is slipping because the edge case work has consumed all my bandwidth", confirm the ticket is overdue and flag the dependency.
OE 11: Retrieve Linear issue details for the launch readiness checklist using `linear_get_issue` (id: issue_launch_readiness_checklist / ENG-219) to check its state. Assigned to Samira, state: todo, due 3/21. This is part of the remediation deliverables for Elena, verify whether it was completed and included in the Friday 3/21 status update.
OE 12: Retrieve Linear issue details for the pilot monitoring ticket using `linear_get_issue` (id: issue_pilot_monitoring / ENG-211) to check its state. Assigned to Samira, state: in_progress, due 3/27. This is the umbrella ticket tracking pilot performance, verify its status given that auto-decisions are now suspended.
OE 13: Retrieve comments on key remediation tickets using `linear_list_comments` (issueId) for ENG-211 (issue_pilot_monitoring), ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config), ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed), ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses), ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening), and ENG-212 (issue_edge_cases_backlog). These comments contain the strongest evidence of actual work completion: Lena's deploy verification on ENG-215 (comment_lena_deploy_verified, 3/18), Dmitri's full audit results posted on ENG-217 (comment_dmitri_audit_results, 3/20), Dmitri's 6 schema validation tests confirmed on ENG-212 (comment_dmitri_validation_tests, 3/20), and Samira's remediation plan with ticket assignments on ENG-211 (comment_samira_pause_confirmed, 3/18).
OE 14: Search Slack (#engineering) using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "Lena" and "config fix" or "pipeline" or "deployed" or "hardening") to find any updates from Lena confirming that the Vectral/Mosaic config fixes and pipeline hardening work were completed, since the Linear tickets may not have been updated.
OE 15: Search Slack (#engineering) using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "Anh" and "dashboard" or "type validation" or "policy editor") to find whether Anh posted any progress updates on the dashboard type validation work (ENG-218) after the initial acknowledgment, or if it truly went silent as Dmitri suspects.
OE 16: Search Slack (#engineering) using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "edge case" or "validation tests" or "deployment gate" or "schema validation") to find updates on whether Dmitri's edge case validation tests (ENG-212) have been incorporated into the deployment gate as part of Lena's pipeline hardening (ENG-217), or if they are sitting unintegrated.
OE 17: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "Elena" and "status update" or "remediation" or "relaunch" or "readiness") to discover whether Samira sent Elena the Friday 3/21 status update with the launch readiness checklist and audit summary, and whether Elena responded with a sign-off or additional requirements for re-enabling auto-decisions.
OE 18: For each ticket where the actual state of work (per Slack, email, or discussion evidence) differs from the Linear ticket status, update the ticket using `linear_update_issue` (id, state) to reflect the true status. Specifically: (a) if ENG-214 and ENG-215 config fixes are done but tickets show in_progress, move to done; (b) if ENG-216 audit is done but shows in_progress, move to done; (c) for ENG-217 pipeline hardening, verify whether all acceptance criteria are met, including the schema validation gate integration (Dmitri's 6 tests from ENG-212 wired into the deployment pipeline as a blocking step). If evidence confirms all criteria are complete, move to done; if the schema validation gate integration remains unconfirmed (e.g., acceptance criteria checkboxes still unchecked, only future-tense Slack messages about integration), keep in_progress and add a comment documenting what is confirmed done versus what remains unverified; (d) if ENG-218 dashboard validation is stalled with no progress, add a comment flagging the stall and noting it as blocked.
OE 19: For any ticket that is blocked with no explanation (e.g., ENG-218 if Anh hasn't provided a reason, or ENG-220 if it's overdue with no blocker note), add a comment using `linear_create_comment` (issueId) documenting the blocker status, what evidence was found (or not found), and requesting an update from the assignee.
OE 20: Post a summary message to the #engineering Slack channel using `conversations_add_message` (channel_id: C003) providing the reconciled status of all expense categoriser remediation tickets: which are confirmed done, which are still in progress, which are stalled or blocked, and what remains before Samira can commit to a relaunch timeline for auto-decisions.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
